• The mission now highlights trusted collaboration and partnerships in addition to technology leadership.
• Federal advocacy priorities include AI policy respecting state autonomy, cybersecurity grant reauthorization, and FirstNet reauthorization.
• NASCIO aims to serve as a trusted resource and national advocate for state technology leaders.
• The update reflects a broader trend of elevating CIO roles within state government leadership and policy influence.

announcement: Deepgram speech-to-text and voice models now available natively on Together AI
summary:
Deepgram's speech-to-text (STT) and text-to-speech (TTS) models are now available natively on Together AI's platform, enabling a unified production environment for real-time voice agents. This partnership addresses critical challenges in conversational AI such as turn-taking, latency, and operational fragility by co-locating transcription, synthesis, and large language models (LLMs) on one infrastructure. The integration signals a move towards more seamless, low-latency voice AI pipelines that improve natural conversational experiences in enterprise applications. It also highlights growing demand for secure, compliant voice AI solutions with enterprise-grade controls.

• Deepgram's STT and TTS models (Nova-3, Flux, Aura-2) run natively on Together AI's Dedicated Model Inference platform.
• Together AI offers a single production surface combining STT, LLM, and TTS for real-time voice agents.
• Deepgram's Flux model improves conversational turn detection with 250ms end-of-turn latency, reducing reliance on complex endpointing logic.
• Enterprise features include zero data retention, SOC 2 Type II compliance, HIPAA readiness, and data residency options.
• The alliance reduces latency and operational complexity by eliminating network hops between providers.

announcement: AI for Systems: Using LLMs to Optimize Database Query Execution
summary:
A new approach leverages large language models (LLMs) to optimize database query execution, addressing longstanding inefficiencies in traditional cost-based query optimizers. By incorporating semantic awareness and overcoming assumptions like attribute independence, this AI-driven method improves query planning for complex OLAP workloads. The introduction of DBPlanBench enables LLMs to interact with physical query plans, signaling a shift toward AI-enhanced database systems. This innovation highlights AI's expanding role beyond model training into core systems infrastructure optimization.

• LLMs are applied to optimize database query execution plans, improving efficiency.
• Traditional query optimizers struggle with semantic correlations and attribute independence assumptions.
• DBPlanBench exposes internal physical operator graphs to LLMs for enhanced optimization.
• AI-driven semantic-aware join optimization can drastically reduce query execution times.
• Physical query planning remains an open challenge, especially for complex OLAP workloads.

announcement: Wan 2.7 now available on Together AI
summary:
Wan 2.7, a four-model AI video generation and editing suite, has launched on the Together AI platform, starting with text-to-video capabilities. This launch addresses the challenge of fragmented video generation workflows by integrating generation, continuation, reference-driven control, and editing into a unified API platform. The product offers flexible resolution, duration control, and audio-driven generation, signaling a maturation in AI-driven video content creation tools. This integration on Together AI's native cloud platform simplifies developer access and billing, accelerating adoption in creative and marketing applications.

• Wan 2.7 introduces a four-model suite for video generation, continuation, reference-driven workflows, and editing.
• Initial rollout focuses on text-to-video generation with plans to expand to image-to-video, reference-to-video, and video editing.
• The suite offers tighter creative control via audio inputs, frame-level conditioning, and reference matching to reduce workflow fragmentation.
• Available on Together AI's AI Native Cloud with unified APIs, authentication, and billing for seamless developer integration.
• Pricing starts at $0.10 per second of generated video, targeting campaign content, product videos, and creative prototyping.

announcement: What is an AI Native Cloud?
summary:
The article discusses the emergence of AI native companies that build their products around AI models rather than adding AI as a feature to legacy systems. It highlights the need for a new kind of cloud infrastructure purpose-built for AI workloads that supports rapid iteration, continuous model improvement, and efficient GPU usage. This signals a platform shift in cloud computing driven by AI's unique lifecycle and scaling demands. The concept of an AI Native Cloud reflects a strategic evolution in infrastructure to maintain competitive advantage in AI innovation.

• AI native companies treat AI models as core products with rapid iteration cycles.
• Traditional cloud infrastructure optimized for CPU-heavy web apps is insufficient for AI workloads.
• AI Native Cloud must support the entire AI lifecycle: pretraining, fine-tuning, evaluation, and inference.
• Staying close to frontier AI research and integrating innovations continuously is critical.
• The new cloud must optimize for model quality, latency, cost, and reliability at scale.

announcement: Finding inspiration and a bit of hope in Corgispace
summary:
The article reflects on the challenging state of the games industry workforce amid layoffs and studio closures, highlighting a talk by Adam Saltsman at GDC about his CorgiSpace manifesto. This initiative promotes making small, short-cycle games as a creative practice to sustain inspiration and hope during difficult times. Strategically, it signals a focus on creative resilience and mental health support for game developers, emphasizing manageable project scopes to foster innovation and well-being. It underscores the importance of nurturing talent and creative practice as a response to industry contraction and workforce challenges.

• The games industry workforce is facing significant struggles including layoffs and job scarcity.
• Adam Saltsman's CorgiSpace manifesto advocates for short, fun game development cycles to maintain creativity.
• The approach separates core ideas from formulaic patterns to enable easier, more engaging game creation.
• This creative practice offers hope and resilience for developers amid industry contraction.
• The initiative highlights the value of supporting talent mental health and sustainable creative workflows.

announcement: Food is Medicine, Explained
summary:
This publication explains the concept of 'Food is Medicine,' emphasizing the role of food choices in health outcomes and the potential for food system innovation to improve public health. It draws on the author's expertise in food science and legal frameworks to contextualize the intersection of food, health, and policy. The piece aims to inform and inspire stakeholders to consider food as a critical component of healthcare and social well-being. The intended impact is to advance understanding and support for integrating food-based approaches into health policy and practice.

• Food choices significantly influence health and societal outcomes.
• Innovative food system approaches can enhance public health.
• Legal and policy frameworks are important for advancing food-as-medicine initiatives.
• Storytelling is a powerful tool to promote food system innovation.

announcement: Celebrating Farmers, Spotlighting Food System Visionaries
summary:
The publication announces an event hosted by Food Tank in Nairobi, Kenya, focusing on celebrating farmers and highlighting food system visionaries. The event premieres two documentary films on the African continent and gathers experts to discuss pathways for building more resilient and equitable food and farming systems. It emphasizes the role of storytelling in scaling solutions within food systems. The intended impact is to foster dialogue, raise awareness, and inspire action toward sustainable food system transformation.

• The event spotlights farmers and food system visionaries to celebrate their contributions.
• Premieres of documentary films aim to use storytelling to amplify food system challenges and solutions.
• Experts will discuss strategies to build resilient and equitable food and farming systems.
• Partnerships with organizations like Enviu and The Rockefeller Foundation highlight collaborative approaches.
• The event serves as a platform for agenda-setting in food system transformation in Africa.

announcement: The U.S. and Iran Struck a Two-Week Truce. Now What? | Council on Foreign Relations
summary:
The publication reports on the announcement of a two-week ceasefire between the United States, Iran, and Israel after prolonged conflict, mediated by Pakistan. It highlights the fragile nature of the truce, ongoing military tensions, and the initial diplomatic steps toward negotiations in Islamabad. The brief underscores the mixed reactions from regional and global actors, the economic impact on oil markets, and the uncertainty about whether this ceasefire will lead to a lasting peace settlement. The intended impact is to inform policymakers and stakeholders about the current status and complexities of the ceasefire and the geopolitical dynamics involved.

• A fragile two-week ceasefire was agreed upon between the U.S., Iran, and Israel, mediated by Pakistan.
• Disagreements remain, particularly regarding the inclusion of Lebanon in the agreement and ongoing attacks.
• The ceasefire includes plans to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical shipping route, though traffic has not yet increased significantly.
• Economic markets responded positively with oil futures dropping and stock markets rising, but supply chain disruptions remain a concern.
• The U.S. and Iran are invited to begin negotiations in Islamabad, signaling a potential diplomatic pathway forward.

announcement: PODCAST | The President’s Inbox: Russia’s Stakes in the Iran War, With Thomas Graham | Council on Foreign Relations
summary:
This podcast episode from the Council on Foreign Relations analyzes the complex and evolving relationship between Russia and Iran amid the ongoing Iran conflict. Thomas Graham explains that while Russia and Iran share some strategic interests, particularly in countering U.S. sanctions and influence, their relationship remains cautious and competitive rather than a formal alliance. The discussion highlights Moscow's reluctance to commit militarily to Iran, the historical rivalry between the two nations, and the broader geopolitical implications for U.S.-Russia relations and regional security. The analysis aims to inform policymakers and the public about the nuanced dynamics shaping the Russia-Iran nexus and its impact on global diplomacy.

• Russia and Iran have a historically complicated relationship characterized as 'frenemies' rather than allies.
• Both countries cooperate to circumvent U.S. sanctions but maintain mutual suspicions and compete economically, especially in energy markets.
• Russia's strategic partnership with Iran lacks a defense clause, indicating no obligation to militarily support Iran in the current conflict.
• Moscow perceives the U.S. administration as deprioritizing Russia, which influences its approach to Iran and regional conflicts.
• The Russia-Iran relationship affects broader geopolitical issues, including the war in Ukraine and U.S.-Russia diplomatic relations.

announcement: South Africa’s “Other” Problem | Council on Foreign Relations
summary:
The publication analyzes violent reactions in South Africa to the installation of a traditional Igbo king by the Nigerian diaspora, interpreting the conflict as a manifestation of broader anti-immigrant sentiment rather than a genuine cultural dispute. It highlights how local leaders and populist movements use appeals to sovereignty and traditional values as a pretext for xenophobic actions against foreign nationals, particularly Nigerians. The piece situates these events within the rise of populist, anti-immigrant groups in South Africa, signaling ongoing social and political tensions around immigration and national identity.

• The installation of a traditional Igbo king in South Africa triggered violent backlash rooted in xenophobia, not cultural offense.
• Local traditional leaders and populist groups frame immigrant presence as a threat to sovereignty and traditional values.
• Anti-immigrant movements like Operation Dudula and March and March are increasingly active and influential in South Africa.
• The conflict reflects deeper societal unease with undocumented immigration and foreign economic participation.
• Government responses to such tensions are critical to managing social cohesion and immigrant rights.

announcement: How I Got My Career in Foreign Policy: Carla Anne Robbins | Council on Foreign Relations
summary:
This publication profiles Carla Anne Robbins' career trajectory in foreign policy journalism, highlighting how her academic background and early experiences shaped her approach to international reporting. It emphasizes the importance of rigorous research skills and direct engagement with global political actors in developing expertise. The piece aims to inspire and inform aspiring foreign policy professionals about diverse career pathways and the value of immersive journalism. It also underscores the role of institutions like the Council on Foreign Relations in fostering expertise and education in international affairs.

• Academic training in international affairs provides essential research skills for journalism.
• Direct engagement and asking primary sources is crucial for understanding foreign policy decisions.
• Career paths in foreign policy can span journalism, academia, and think tank fellowship roles.
• Institutions like the Council on Foreign Relations play a key role in career development and knowledge dissemination.

announcement: As a Strait of Hormuz Standoff Grows, Will Trump’s Fragile Iran Ceasefire Hold? | Council on Foreign Relations
summary:
The publication analyzes the fragile two-week ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran amid escalating conflict in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global energy supplies. It highlights the conditional nature of the ceasefire, focusing on Iran's reopening of the strait and cessation of uranium enrichment as key terms, while noting Iran's contradictory actions and skepticism from experts about the prospects for long-term peace. The brief underscores the geopolitical risks to global energy markets and regional security, emphasizing the precarious balance of military and diplomatic pressures. It aims to inform policymakers and stakeholders about the complexities and potential consequences of the ceasefire's enforcement.

• The ceasefire hinges on Iran's complete and immediate reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and halting uranium enrichment.
• Iran's subsequent closure of the strait and ongoing regional hostilities cast doubt on the ceasefire's durability.
• U.S. military strikes have significantly degraded Iran's military capabilities but readiness to resume offensive actions remains.
• Scheduled negotiations in Islamabad face skepticism regarding their ability to achieve substantive regime or policy changes in Iran.
• The conflict's disruption of global energy markets underscores the strategic importance of the Strait of Hormuz.

announcement: China in the Indo-Pacific: December 2025 | Council on Foreign Relations
summary:
This publication analyzes China's recent military drills around Taiwan and escalating tensions with Japan, highlighting China's strategic intent to deter external support for Taiwan and isolate Japan diplomatically. It documents China's extensive military exercises, including live-fire drills and coordinated naval and air operations, alongside diplomatic efforts to pressure European countries to uphold the One China principle. The brief also notes Japan's response and U.S. support, framing the regional security environment as increasingly fraught with military signaling and diplomatic contestation. The analysis underscores the interplay between military posturing and diplomatic isolation efforts as China seeks to assert dominance in the Indo-Pacific.

• China conducted its largest military drills around Taiwan to signal deterrence against independence and external interference.
• Tensions between China and Japan escalated with radar targeting incidents and conflicting maritime sovereignty claims near disputed islands.
• The U.S. demonstrated support for Japan through joint military exercises and diplomatic criticism of China’s actions.
• China intensified diplomatic efforts to isolate Japan by appealing to European countries to uphold the One China principle.
• The symbolic end of panda loans to Japan marks a soft power setback amid deteriorating bilateral relations.

announcement: Trump Secured a Ceasefire With Iran. Will It Last? | Council on Foreign Relations
summary:
The publication analyzes the recent ceasefire agreement between the United States and Iran announced by former President Trump, highlighting the complexities and challenges that may undermine its durability. It scrutinizes the conflicting positions on Iran's demands, including uranium enrichment rights, sanctions relief, and control over the Strait of Hormuz, which clash with longstanding U.S. red lines. The analysis underscores the fragile nature of the truce, the diplomatic ambiguities surrounding its implementation, and the geopolitical implications for U.S. allies in the region. The piece aims to provide context on the ceasefire's prospects and the strategic stakes involved.

• Trump's declaration of a total U.S. victory in securing the ceasefire is premature given unresolved contentious issues.
• Iran's peace plan includes demands that conflict with U.S. strategic red lines, such as uranium enrichment and control of the Strait of Hormuz.
• Recognition of Iran's control over the Strait of Hormuz would represent a strategic setback for the U.S. and its regional allies.
• The ceasefire is fragile with unclear terms and potential for derailment amid diplomatic confusion and regional tensions.

announcement: Federal AI policy needs labor-centric approach, lawmakers tell White House
summary:
Two bipartisan U.S. lawmakers urged the White House to adopt a labor-focused approach in federal AI policy, emphasizing the importance of protecting and empowering American workers amid AI adoption. They referenced AFL-CIO's principles advocating for worker involvement, transparency, retraining, and collective bargaining in AI deployment. This initiative signals a push to balance technological innovation with workforce rights and long-term economic competitiveness. It highlights the political and social dimensions of AI governance, especially regarding labor's role in shaping AI's impact on jobs and workplace dynamics.

• Bipartisan lawmakers called for AI policies that prioritize American workers' interests and involvement.
• They endorsed AFL-CIO's nonpartisan principles emphasizing transparency, accountability, retraining, and collective bargaining.
• The letter stresses AI's development must include meaningful worker input to ensure fairness and competitiveness.
• The lawmakers advocate for federal AI policy to maintain human judgment and improve job quality and safety.
• Immediate administrative actions are urged alongside ongoing legislative efforts to embed these labor-centric commitments.

announcement: De Tokio para el mundo: Mira el concierto Billions Club Live de Bad Bunny en Spotify — Spotify
summary:
Spotify has launched an exclusive concert film featuring Bad Bunny's historic first performance in Asia, recorded in Tokyo. This release expands Spotify's live event content offering to a global audience, enhancing fan engagement across multiple regions and devices. The initiative signals Spotify's strategic push to broaden its market reach through immersive music experiences and localized fan events worldwide. It also highlights the growing trend of digital live entertainment as a key growth vector in music streaming platforms.

• Bad Bunny's first live concert in Asia was filmed and released exclusively on Spotify.
• The concert film is accessible globally on multiple platforms with multilingual subtitles.
• Spotify organized fan experiences in Latin American cities to celebrate the launch.
• The initiative strengthens artist-to-fan connections through unique digital content.
• This move represents Spotify's expansion into live event streaming and global content localization.

announcement: From Tokyo to the World: Watch Bad Bunny’s Billions Club Live Concert on Spotify — Spotify
summary:
Spotify premiered Bad Bunny's first-ever live concert performance in Asia, filmed in Tokyo, as a global concert film accessible on multiple devices worldwide. This initiative signals Spotify's strategic push to expand its live entertainment content and deepen artist-fan engagement across new geographic markets. By localizing content with multilingual subtitles and hosting fan experiences in Latin American cities, Spotify is leveraging cultural and regional connectivity to grow its global audience. This move highlights the growing importance of immersive, localized digital music experiences in the streaming industry.

• Bad Bunny performed his first live concert in Asia, in Tokyo, on March 7.
• Spotify released an exclusive 42-minute concert film globally on its platform.
• The film includes multilingual subtitles to reach diverse international audiences.
• Spotify hosted fan viewing events with exclusive merchandise in Latin America.
• This effort enhances Spotify's global market presence and artist-fan connection.

announcement: FedEx Reaches Tentative Agreement with Pilots
summary:
FedEx and the Air Line Pilots Association (ALPA) have reached a tentative collective bargaining agreement for over 5,000 pilots, pending ratification. This agreement aims to provide industry-leading terms that support both pilot welfare and FedEx's broader growth strategy. The deal signals a strategic commitment to workforce stability and operational continuity, which is critical for maintaining service quality and customer satisfaction globally. It also reflects effective labor relations management involving key stakeholders including the National Mediation Board.

• FedEx and ALPA reached a tentative agreement for pilot contracts covering 5,000+ pilots.
• The agreement is positioned as industry-leading and supports FedEx's growth strategy.
• National Mediation Board and professional mediators played a key role in negotiations.
• The deal aims to benefit pilots, FedEx operations, and customers worldwide.

announcement: FedEx Freight Hosts Inaugural Investor Day Ahead of Planned Spinoff from FedEx
summary:
FedEx Freight held its inaugural Investor Day at the NYSE to introduce itself as an independent public company ahead of its planned spinoff from FedEx Corporation. The event highlighted FedEx Freight's position as the largest North American less-than-truckload (LTL) carrier and outlined its strategic priorities focused on network optimization, commercial offerings, technology advancement, and financial value creation. This move signals a strategic shift to unlock shareholder value through focused growth, operational efficiency, and targeted investments in technology. The spinoff enables FedEx Freight to operate with greater flexibility and a renewed focus on its competitive advantages in the LTL market.

• FedEx Freight plans to spin off from FedEx Corporation and become an independent public company.
• The company is the largest pure-play LTL carrier in North America with a strong market position.
• Four strategic priorities include network optimization, differentiated commercial offerings, technology modernization, and disciplined financial management.
• Technology investments will focus on automation, AI, data analytics, and digital customer experience enhancements.
• The spinoff aims to accelerate profitable growth and unlock full potential with greater operational focus.

announcement: UCF research to help inform statewide efforts to stop domestic violence and improve care for survivors
summary:
Researchers at UCF, funded by a Florida Partnership grant, are conducting a comprehensive statewide assessment of domestic violence needs. The project involves multidisciplinary faculty analyzing data and community input to identify gaps in prevention and survivor support services. The findings aim to inform policymakers and local agencies to improve funding strategies and empower survivors, especially in rapidly changing demographic areas. This research supports long-term, practical improvements in domestic violence prevention and care across Florida.

• UCF researchers are conducting a two-year statewide domestic violence needs assessment funded by a $257,384 grant.
• The study integrates expertise across social work, education, criminal justice, sociology, and medicine.
• Findings will help policymakers and agencies develop better strategies to fund and support prevention programs.
• The research highlights challenges survivors face, including economic strain and impacts of population changes on service needs.
• The project emphasizes creating accessible, practitioner-friendly research tools for sustained use.

announcement: Healthier plant-based diet associated with lower risk of Alzheimer’s, other dementias
summary:
A large observational study involving nearly 93,000 diverse participants found that eating a higher quality plant-based diet is associated with a lower risk of Alzheimer's disease and other dementias. The study differentiated between healthful plant-based diets rich in whole grains, fruits, vegetables, nuts, and legumes, and unhealthful plant-based diets high in refined grains, added sugars, and processed foods. While the research shows association rather than causation, it suggests that improving plant-based diet quality, even later in life, may reduce dementia risk. These findings could inform dietary guidance and public health strategies aimed at cognitive health.

• Higher quality plant-based diets are linked to reduced risk of Alzheimer's and other dementias.
• Unhealthful plant-based diets, including processed and sugary plant foods, are linked to increased dementia risk.
• Diet quality changes over 10 years correlate with corresponding changes in dementia risk.
• The study is observational and does not prove causation between diet and dementia risk.
• Adopting a healthful plant-based diet even after age 60 is associated with lower dementia risk.

announcement: Genetic markers fast-track breeding of seedless muscadine grapes
summary:
Researchers developed and validated genetic markers that can predict seedlessness and flower sex type in muscadine grapes years before fruiting. This was achieved by genotyping over 1,100 grape samples using a low-cost platform targeting known mutations linked to these traits. The markers are publicly available and can accelerate breeding of seedless, self-pollinating muscadine and other grape varieties. This advancement promises to save time and resources in developing flavorful, disease-resistant grapes for wine and table use.

• Genetic markers enable early prediction of seedlessness and flower sex in muscadine grapes.
• The study validated markers in over 900 hybrid and 200 cultivated/wild grape samples.
• Markers are low-cost and publicly accessible for global grape breeding programs.
• Collaboration involved University of Arkansas, Cornell, USDA, and industry partners.
• Research was supported by USDA Specialty Crop Research Initiative grants.

announcement: Reported 2025 drug overdose ‘spike’ was an illusion, new study finds
summary:
A Northwestern University study revealed that a reported spike in U.S. drug overdose deaths in early 2025 was a statistical artifact caused by predictive modeling of lagged data, not an actual increase. The researchers analyzed CDC provisional mortality data and identified that models struggled to adapt to rapidly changing overdose trends after a prolonged peak. This finding corrects public misconceptions about data manipulation and highlights the need for improved surveillance systems during epidemic turning points. Accurate overdose data is critical for guiding public health policy and resource allocation.

• The January 2025 reported spike in overdose deaths was a modeling artifact, not a real increase.
• Overdose deaths have continued to decline since their peak in August 2023, marking a historic sustained decrease.
• CDC provisional data models struggled to adapt to rapid shifts in overdose trends, causing temporary overestimation.
• The study dispels conspiracy theories about CDC data manipulation or political interference.
• Surveillance systems need enhancement to better handle dynamic epidemic trend changes.

announcement: Linker histone H1 acts as a liquid-like “glue” for chromatin
summary:
This research overturns the traditional view that linker histone H1 compacts chromatin by forming rigid, ordered structures. Using single-nucleosome imaging, super-resolution fluorescence microscopy, and computational modeling, the team demonstrated that H1 binds dynamically to nucleosomes and acts like a liquid-like glue. This dynamic binding compacts chromatin into a fluid, accessible organization rather than a static fiber, which better supports essential genome functions. These insights deepen understanding of genome architecture and may impact fields studying gene regulation and chromatin dynamics.

• Linker histone H1 does not form rigid, static chromatin fibers as previously thought.
• H1 binds nucleosomes dynamically, acting as a liquid-like glue to compact chromatin.
• Chromatin organization is fluid and dynamic, maintaining accessibility for genome functions.
• Advanced imaging and computational modeling were key methods enabling this discovery.
• The findings challenge longstanding textbook models of chromatin structure.

announcement: University of Minnesota researchers develop new method providing clearer view of how genome functions in cancer
summary:
University of Minnesota researchers developed PARTAGE, a novel method that simultaneously measures DNA replication timing, DNA copy number changes, and gene activity from the same sample. This integrated approach overcomes traditional limitations where these genomic features were studied separately, enabling a clearer understanding of genome regulation and disruption in cancer. PARTAGE matches the accuracy of existing gold-standard methods and reveals links between early DNA replication and active gene expression, as well as precise detection of genomic alterations in cancer cells. The method holds promise for identifying new biomarkers and therapeutic targets in cancer research.

• PARTAGE enables simultaneous measurement of DNA replication timing, copy number variation, and gene expression from a single sample.
• The method produces results as accurate as traditional separate assays.
• PARTAGE reveals a strong correlation between early DNA replication and active gene expression.
• It precisely detects genomic alterations such as DNA gains or losses in cancer cells.
• The approach could facilitate discovery of new cancer biomarkers and therapeutic targets.

announcement: Global musicians face the same 'streaming paradox' as US- and UK-based artists, study finds
summary:
A study surveying approximately 1,200 musicians across Brazil, Chile, the Netherlands, Nigeria, and South Korea reveals a 'streaming paradox' where artists gain visibility through streaming platforms but struggle to earn meaningful income. The research highlights significant variations in musicians' experiences based on country, genre, and generation, emphasizing the influence of cultural and economic contexts. The findings also show that musicians spend substantial time on promotion and audience management, with limited use of AI tools. This study informs understanding of the evolving digital music economy and its impact on artists worldwide.

• 77% of musicians earn less than €10,000 annually from music, with 83% dissatisfied with streaming royalties.
• 81% consider streaming important for their careers, but fewer than half report improved situations since streaming dominance.
• Musicians in Nigeria report more positive career impacts from streaming (83%) compared to the Netherlands (14%).
• Nearly a quarter of musicians spend over half their work time on promotion and fan engagement, rising to 30% in Chile.
• 89% of musicians do not use AI or automation tools for online fan interaction, with Dutch artists most concerned about AI-generated music competition.

announcement: World's largest study of human flourishing opens its data to the public
summary:
The Global Flourishing Study (GFS), the largest empirical investigation into human flourishing, has publicly released its first two waves of data, covering about 200,000 participants across 22 countries. The study measures flourishing across six domains including happiness, health, meaning, character, relationships, and financial stability. By surveying the same participants over multiple waves, it enables causal insights beyond simple correlations. This open data resource supports researchers, policymakers, and the public in understanding and improving human well-being worldwide.

• GFS is the most comprehensive study on human flourishing with over 100 peer-reviewed publications.
• Data covers six key domains of flourishing: happiness, health, meaning, character, relationships, and financial stability.
• Longitudinal design allows for causal analysis rather than just correlations.
• Findings highlight youth well-being challenges, the paradox of wealth and purpose, and the positive role of faith communities.
• Data is freely accessible with some sensitive variables requiring IRB approval; Wave 3 data available upon preregistration.

announcement: A “stemness checkpoint” helps control stem cell identity
summary:
Researchers identified the protein GSK3α as a key 'stemness checkpoint' that controls the identity of various stem cell types across developmental stages. By inhibiting GSK3α, mouse embryonic and epiblast stem cells maintained their distinct identities and self-renewal even when co-cultured. This finding challenges the previous view that stem cell maintenance depends on many unrelated signals, proposing instead shared checkpoints across stem cell states. The discovery could improve laboratory stem cell maintenance, aiding development studies, disease modeling, drug testing, and regenerative medicine.

• GSK3α acts as a common checkpoint regulating stem cell differentiation and identity.
• Inhibition of GSK3α maintains stable self-renewal and identity in different stem cell types.
• Stem cell maintenance involves shared checkpoints rather than unrelated signaling conditions.
• The study used mouse embryonic and epiblast stem cells cultured together under GSK3α inhibition.
• This conceptual framework may enhance stem cell culture methods for biomedical applications.

announcement: Self-employed Hispanic women may be at lower risk for cardiovascular disease compared with their salaried counterparts
summary:
New research shows that self-employed Hispanic women have lower risks of cardiovascular disease-related health issues compared to those employed for wages. The study analyzed data from over 165,000 Hispanic working women collected between 2003 and 2022, focusing on self-reported health outcomes and behaviors. Findings suggest that the structure of work, independent of income or healthcare access, may influence heart disease risk factors in this population. This insight could inform targeted public health strategies and workplace interventions to reduce cardiovascular disease disparities.

• Self-employed Hispanic women report significantly lower rates of diabetes, hypertension, obesity, binge drinking, and poor overall health compared to salaried Hispanic women.
• Self-employed women were about 11% more likely to engage in exercise than their salaried counterparts.
• The association between self-employment and reduced cardiovascular risk factors is not explained by differences in income or healthcare access.
• The reduction in high blood pressure linked to self-employment is nearly as large as that linked to being in the highest income group.
• Study limitations include reliance on self-reported data and lack of occupational detail, and causality cannot be established.

announcement: The Fellowship That Taught Me Good Teaching Doesn’t Require Perfection - EdSurge News
summary:
The Voices of Change fellowship empowered educators to embrace vulnerability and authenticity in their teaching and storytelling, emphasizing that perfection is not required for effective teaching. This initiative encourages teachers to share their unique experiences and perspectives, fostering deeper connections with students and promoting inclusive educational practices. Strategically, it signals a shift toward valuing teacher individuality and emotional presence as key components of workforce development in education.

• The fellowship encourages teachers to embrace their flaws and authentic voices rather than striving for perfection.
• Participants learn to use personal storytelling to enhance their teaching and writing, fostering vulnerability and connection.
• The program highlights the importance of neurodivergence and diversity as strengths in education.
• Teachers gain confidence and empathy, improving their engagement with students on both challenging and successful days.

announcement: How Teachers Make Writing Achievable Without Lowering Standards - EdSurge News
summary:
Educators and Curriculum Associates emphasize the importance of explicit, structured writing instruction to help students overcome common writing challenges without lowering standards. The approach centers on backward design, where teachers clarify the final writing product and plan incremental lessons to build skills systematically. This signals a strategic shift in education toward research-based pedagogy that addresses cognitive load and foundational skill gaps in writing. It highlights workforce development in teaching methods to improve student outcomes in literacy.

• Students often struggle with writing due to cognitive demands and gaps in foundational skills.
• Effective writing instruction requires explicit teaching of writing craft, not just assigning essays.
• Backward design helps teachers plan coherent lessons focused on clear final writing goals.
• This approach supports higher standards while making writing achievable for diverse learners.

announcement: Some Advocates Concerned As States Push for Cameras in Special Education Classrooms - EdSurge News
summary:
Several U.S. states are introducing legislation to install surveillance cameras in self-contained special education classrooms to increase accountability and protect students with special needs from harmful physical practices. This initiative aims to provide transparency and safeguard both students and educators, though it raises privacy concerns among some disability advocates. The movement reflects a broader debate on the ethical use of technology in education, balancing safety and privacy. The trend is gaining momentum as technology becomes more affordable and accessible for classroom monitoring.

• Multiple states have introduced or adopted bills to install cameras in special education classrooms.
• The legislation aims to prevent abuse and increase accountability in classrooms serving students with special needs.
• There is support from many parents but also concerns about privacy and the potential stigmatization of students.
• The initiative is part of a larger debate on technology use and ethics in educational settings.
• Technology affordability and availability are enabling wider adoption of classroom surveillance.

announcement: Why the Social Media Addiction Case Isn’t Over Yet - EdSurge News
summary:
A California jury found Meta and Google negligent in designing social media apps that contribute to youth mental health issues, marking a significant legal precedent. The case focused on addictive design elements like algorithms and beauty filters that engage tweens and teens extensively. This verdict signals increased legal scrutiny on tech companies' responsibility for user well-being, especially minors, and may influence future product design and regulatory frameworks. With ongoing appeals, the case highlights evolving challenges in balancing platform innovation with social responsibility.

• Jury found Meta and Google negligent for social media design impacting youth mental health.
• Internal emails revealed company awareness of risks but prioritized user engagement.
• Snapchat and TikTok parent companies settled prior to trial.
• The verdict could affect thousands of similar lawsuits and influence tech feature rollouts.
• Legal protections like Section 230 are being challenged in the context of design liability.

announcement: Most Active And Highest-Spending Startup Investors Diverged In Q1
summary:
In Q1, the most active startup investors by deal count differed significantly from those writing the largest checks, highlighting a divergence in investment strategies. High-profile AI startups like OpenAI and Anthropic attracted massive funding rounds led by less frequent but deep-pocketed investors such as D.E. Shaw and MGX. Meanwhile, prolific investors like Andreessen Horowitz and Accel led numerous smaller rounds, emphasizing volume over deal size. This dynamic signals a bifurcation in venture capital approaches amid record-setting funding rounds, especially in AI sectors.

• Top investors by deal count were not the same as those funding the largest rounds in Q1.
• OpenAI and Anthropic secured mega-rounds totaling over $150 billion, led by D.E. Shaw and MGX.
• Andreessen Horowitz and Accel were the most active lead investors by number of post-seed rounds.
• Y Combinator led the highest number of post-seed and seed-stage deals, including follow-ons.
• Tech giants Nvidia and Amazon participated as strategic investors in OpenAI's record round.

announcement: NRF | Global conflicts and their expanding impact on enterprise security risk
summary:
The announcement highlights the expanding impact of global conflicts on retail enterprise security risks, emphasizing the need for retailers to integrate physical security, cybersecurity, asset protection, and crisis response into a unified Enterprise Security Risk Management (ESRM) framework. It underscores how geopolitical tensions increase risks such as workplace violence, civil unrest, and cyberattacks from state and non-state actors targeting retail infrastructure. Strategically, it signals a shift in retail security from a tactical function to a core organizational capability that supports resilience, operational stability, and reputation management amid global instability.

• Global conflicts increase risks of customer aggression, workplace violence, and civil unrest affecting retail operations.
• Retailers face challenges balancing employee convictions with organizational neutrality while maintaining safety and inclusivity.
• Cyber threats from state-sponsored actors exploit geopolitical tensions to target retail systems, risking operational downtime.
• Enterprise Security Risk Management (ESRM) integrates physical and cyber security with crisis response to build resilience.
• Security leadership roles are evolving to become strategic enablers rather than cost centers in retail organizations.

announcement: American-made FranklinWH Energy Storage keeps manufacturing local
summary:
FranklinWH, a Silicon Valley-based residential energy storage company founded in 2019, has rapidly grown to become a top battery brand in the U.S. market by offering American-made battery systems that provide homeowners energy control and freedom. The company emphasizes local manufacturing in California, achieving record-breaking permit-to-production timelines and onboarding a large certified installer network. This initiative signals a strategic focus on reshoring manufacturing, accelerating market penetration, and promoting energy independence through advanced residential battery solutions.

• FranklinWH is the third-most quoted residential battery brand on EnergySage, behind Tesla and Enphase.
• The company started manufacturing in California within five months of receiving permits, leveraging experienced third-party contractors.
• FranklinWH’s flagship aPower system supports multiple power sources, offers a 15-year warranty, and promotes 'energy freedom' for homeowners.
• The company onboarded 1,000 U.S. contractors within one year and ranked eighth in U.S. battery installations by year two.
• Local manufacturing in the U.S. is a core strategic element, reinforcing the brand’s commitment to American-made products.

announcement: Enervus launches AI platform for project workflow execution
summary:
Enverus has launched Enverus ONE, an AI-powered platform designed to unify and automate workflows for energy professionals across multiple sectors including upstream, midstream, power, renewables, and capital markets. The platform integrates frontier AI models with Enverus’ proprietary energy model Astra, providing domain-specific intelligence that enhances decision-making and operational execution. This initiative signals a strategic move to increase operational efficiency and reliability in energy workflows by leveraging AI tailored to the energy industry's unique complexities. It positions Enverus as a leader in AI-driven workflow automation within the energy sector, potentially creating a competitive gap between early adopters and laggards.

• Enverus launched Enverus ONE, an AI platform combining frontier AI with proprietary energy modeling.
• The platform supports workflow execution across upstream, midstream, power, renewables, and capital markets.
• Astra model provides domain-specific context enabling reliable evaluation and decision-ready outputs.
• Built on trusted proprietary data used by over 8,000 energy companies, enhancing precision over time.
• CEO emphasizes the platform as a critical operational layer that will differentiate fast-moving companies.

announcement: Sitetracker unveils Scout AI platform
summary:
Sitetracker has launched Scout, an agentic AI platform designed to automate and optimize workflows across the asset lifecycle for infrastructure owners, operators, and contractors. By integrating data from multiple sources, Scout enables automated work packages that enhance productivity from planning through maintenance. This platform aims to compress timelines, drive automation, and improve margins, signaling a strategic push toward AI-driven operational efficiency in infrastructure management. Scout's limited release precedes a full launch planned for spring, indicating a phased market introduction.

• Sitetracker introduced Scout, an agentic AI platform for infrastructure asset management.
• Scout automates workflows such as document processing, photo intelligence, and risk analysis.
• The platform supports end-to-end asset lifecycle phases: planning, development, construction, and maintenance.
• Scout aims to drive automation, compress timelines, and expand operational margins.
• Scout is currently in limited release with full availability expected this spring.

announcement: Illinois county courthouse cuts ribbon on 1.3-MW solar project
summary:
Cook County in Illinois has completed a 1.3-MW solar installation at its Skokie courthouse as part of its broader Clean Energy Plan. This project supports the county's goals to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, achieve carbon neutrality by 2050, and power all county facilities with 100% renewable electricity by 2030. The initiative demonstrates a strategic commitment to sustainability, cost savings through energy efficiency, and leveraging government incentives. It signals a growing public sector focus on clean energy adoption and climate leadership.

• Completion of a 1.3-MW solar project at the Skokie courthouse in Cook County, Illinois
• Part of Cook County's Clean Energy Plan targeting 45% emissions reduction by 2030 and carbon neutrality by 2050
• Additional 17 solar projects underway to increase renewable energy usage to 32% across county buildings
• Solar installations combined with energy efficiency upgrades to reduce pollution and operating costs
• Recognition of Cook County's progress with a 2025 NACo Achievement Award for surpassing interim emissions goals

announcement: EG4, OutBack Power offer residential battery leasing programs
summary:
OutBack Power and EG4 Electronics have launched separate residential battery leasing programs that provide homeowners access to professionally owned and managed battery systems with flexible installation options and ongoing services. These programs aim to simplify home energy storage adoption by offering standardized system bundles and eligibility-based incentives, enabling broader access to reliable home backup power. The initiative signals a strategic push to expand market reach in residential energy storage by lowering upfront costs and complexity barriers. This approach supports energy independence and resilience for homeowners while leveraging program-based financing and operational support.

• OutBack Power and EG4 Electronics introduced battery leasing programs for residential customers.
• Programs offer professionally managed battery systems with flexible installation choices.
• Standardized system bundles and incentives simplify adoption and ensure eligibility.
• Energy Access 365 Finance supports monitoring, verification, and operational services.
• Solar pairing is optional, allowing independent adoption of storage solutions.

announcement: JCB Sharpens Its Rental Focus, Announces New Rental-Ready 250T CTL and 25Z Mini Ex
summary:
JCB is strategically focusing on the North American rental market by investing $500 million in a new manufacturing facility in San Antonio dedicated to producing rental fleet equipment. The company introduced new rental-optimized machines, including the 250T compact track loader and 25Z mini excavator, designed for high utilization, ease of operation, and versatility favored by rental operators. This move signals JCB's commitment to capturing rental market share through tailored product offerings and localized production capacity. The upcoming 290T loader further emphasizes JCB's rental-centric product development approach.

• JCB is investing $500 million in a new 1 million sq ft manufacturing facility in San Antonio focused on rental equipment production.
• New rental-ready machines launched include the 250T compact track loader and 25Z mini excavator, designed for rental fleet needs.
• The 250T features a 74-horsepower engine, side-door entry, and enhanced visibility for novice operators, aligning with rental user requirements.
• JCB plans to release the more powerful 290T compact track loader in 2026, continuing the rental-focused product expansion.

announcement: Soaring gas prices ripple through retail supply chains
summary:
The US-Israeli war with Iran has caused a sharp increase in gas and diesel prices, significantly impacting retail supply chains through higher fuel surcharges by major parcel carriers like FedEx and UPS. This geopolitical disruption has led consulting firm Kearney to revise its global supply cost inflation forecast upward, signaling immediate cost pressures for companies. Delivery platforms such as DoorDash are responding with driver relief programs to offset rising fuel expenses. Strategically, this highlights the vulnerability of retail logistics to energy price volatility and geopolitical risks, emphasizing the need for operational resilience and cost management.

• Kearney raised 2026 global supply cost inflation forecast from 2.3%-4% to 5%-7% due to energy price spikes.
• FedEx and UPS have significantly increased fuel surcharges indexed to US Department of Energy diesel prices above $5/gallon.
• Diesel and regular gas prices have surged over 30% year-over-year, one of the fastest recent increases.
• DoorDash introduced cash back and relief payments to help delivery drivers cope with higher fuel costs.
• Energy cost increases are directly and immediately passed through to commercial contracts, unlike tariffs.

announcement: Try notebooks in Gemini to easily keep track of projects
summary:
Google's Gemini app has introduced a new feature called notebooks, designed to help users organize chats and files for complex projects. These notebooks act as personal knowledge bases that sync across the Gemini app and NotebookLM, enhancing workflows by allowing users to add custom instructions and relevant files for richer AI context. The rollout begins with Google AI Ultra, Pro, and Plus subscribers on the web, signaling a push towards more integrated AI-powered productivity tools. This development highlights the growing trend of AI-assisted knowledge management and personalized research workflows.

• Gemini app launched notebooks to organize chats and files for complex projects
• Notebooks sync across Gemini and NotebookLM for seamless access
• Users can add custom instructions and files to provide context to Gemini AI
• Feature is initially available to Google AI Ultra, Pro, and Plus web subscribers
• This enhances AI-powered personal knowledge management and productivity

announcement: Claude Managed Agents: get to production 10x faster | Claude
summary:
Claude has launched Claude Managed Agents, a suite of composable APIs designed to accelerate the building and deployment of cloud-hosted AI agents at scale. This product reduces the operational overhead and infrastructure complexity traditionally required, enabling teams to move from prototype to production up to 10x faster. It supports secure sandboxing, multi-agent coordination, and trusted governance, tailored specifically for Claude's AI models. This launch signals a maturation in AI orchestration platforms, emphasizing production readiness and autonomous agent workflows.

• Launch of Claude Managed Agents, enabling 10x faster production deployment of AI agents.
• Managed Agents handle infrastructure complexities like sandboxing, authentication, and permissioning.
• Supports long-running autonomous sessions and multi-agent coordination for complex workflows.
• Built specifically to optimize Claude AI models with self-evaluation and iteration capabilities.
• Integrated session tracing and analytics for debugging and governance within the Claude Console.

announcement: How Carta Healthcare built clinical AI with Claude | Claude
summary:
Carta Healthcare has achieved 10x growth over the past three years, now supporting over 125 hospitals with its Lighthouse clinical data abstraction platform powered by Claude AI via Amazon Bedrock. The startup addresses the complex challenge of converting unstructured clinical records into standardized registry data, automating the nuanced clinical reasoning traditionally performed by skilled human abstractors. This milestone signals growing adoption of AI-powered clinical data management solutions that improve healthcare quality benchmarking and operational efficiency. It also highlights the importance of context-aware AI models in scaling clinical AI applications beyond pilot phases.

• Carta Healthcare grew 10x in three years, supporting 125+ hospitals
• Lighthouse platform uses Claude AI in Amazon Bedrock for clinical data abstraction
• Automates complex clinical reasoning tasks traditionally done by human abstractors
• Addresses challenges of unstructured and variable clinical documentation
• Demonstrates scalable AI application in healthcare data management

company: Center for Climate and Energy Solutions
announcement: Texas Resolve: Lessons from Resilience Efforts in South Central Texas
summary:
The publication highlights a multi-year collaborative effort convened by C2ES to address interconnected climate hazards—extreme heat, wildfire, and flooding—in South Central Texas. It emphasizes community-driven, low-cost resilience strategies that do not rely on large budgets or federal funding, showcasing practical examples from local governments, businesses, and nonprofits. The intended impact is to inspire other regions to adopt holistic, locally-led resilience approaches that address cascading climate risks and their social and economic consequences.

• Extreme heat, wildfire, and flooding pose interconnected and cascading risks to South Central Texas communities.
• Resilience efforts can start without large budgets or federal grants through creative, locally-driven initiatives.
• Cross-sector collaboration among government, academia, business, and community organizations is essential.
• Addressing climate hazards in isolation is insufficient; holistic, community-based approaches are necessary.
• Locally-grown resilience models provide a replicable blueprint for other regions facing climate threats.

announcement: Xcel Minnesota is building a first-of-its-kind virtual power plant
summary:
Xcel Energy Minnesota has received regulatory approval to build and operate the first utility-owned virtual power plant (VPP) in the U.S., deploying up to 200 megawatts of distributed batteries at customer sites. This initiative represents a strategic shift by a utility to directly invest in and control distributed energy resources, aiming to defer costly grid investments and optimize existing infrastructure. The program requires establishing a new metric to value distributed energy resources, potentially setting a precedent for broader utility adoption of VPPs. This move signals a regulatory and market evolution encouraging utilities to integrate distributed energy for grid efficiency and cost reduction.

• Xcel Minnesota approved to deploy up to 200 MW of customer-sited batteries as a utility-owned VPP.
• The $430 million Capacity*Connect program aims to defer expensive grid upgrades by leveraging distributed energy resources.
• Xcel’s direct ownership model contrasts with typical third-party aggregator-led VPPs, giving the utility economic incentives to optimize value.
• The program mandates creation of a valuation metric for distributed energy resources, addressing a key barrier to VPP adoption.
• This initiative could serve as a replicable model for utilities nationwide to integrate DERs and reduce grid costs.

announcement: This program pays nonprofits to take the time to consider solar
summary:
The SUN program in Massachusetts provides financial and technical assistance to nonprofits to evaluate and initiate solar projects before federal tax credits expire. This initiative addresses the unique challenges nonprofits face in adopting solar energy, including limited resources and a tight timeline due to the upcoming expiration of tax incentives. By supporting nonprofits, the program accelerates clean energy adoption in the social sector, signaling a strategic push toward inclusive sustainability and community impact. It builds on a successful model previously applied to affordable housing, demonstrating scalable approaches to decarbonization in underserved sectors.

• SUN program helps nonprofits access solar tax credits before they expire in 2026-2027.
• The program offers financial and technical support to overcome nonprofits' capacity and funding barriers.
• First round exceeded goals with 1.5 MW installed capacity from 5 nonprofits.
• SUN is funded by Massachusetts Clean Energy Center and modeled after the STAR program for affordable housing.
• Nonprofits became eligible for solar tax credits only recently, increasing urgency for adoption.

announcement: Attention Required!
summary:
The announcement informs users that access to a website has been blocked due to security measures triggered by suspicious activity such as certain inputs or malformed data. This reflects the strategic importance of cybersecurity and compliance with online safety protocols to protect digital assets and user data. The notice also guides users on how to resolve access issues, emphasizing transparency and user communication in security enforcement. Overall, it signals the growing emphasis on automated security defenses in web operations to mitigate cyber threats.

• Website access was blocked due to triggering security measures.
